title: Create an Add field from calculation transformation

blocks.content: The add field from calculation transformation allows you to create a new column by performing a custom calculation on one or more existing fields in your data. Use this when you need to create a new field, such as calculating the total, difference, percentage, or ratio.
To use this transformation, open an existing panel in edit mode on your dashboard.
Click **Add transformation** and select **Add field from calculation**.

Under **Mode**, select how you want your calculation applied. Use the following table as a guideline:

| Mode | When to use |
| --- | --- |
| Binary | Apply a basic binary operation on values in a single row from two selected fields. |
| Unary | Apply a basic unary operation on values in a single row from a selected field. |
| Reduce row | Apply a selected calculation on each row of selected fields independently. |
| Row index | Insert a field with the row index. |
| Cumulative functions | Apply functions on the current row and all preceding rows. |
| Window functions | Apply window functions. |
Select a **Mode**, then choose the fields and **Operation** or **Calculation** for your use case. Give the new field a name in **Alias**.
Click **Save dashboard**, confirm the title, and click **Save**.

The transformation is applied automatically. If it doesn't refresh, click **Refresh** on the panel to verify the visualization looks as intended.

In the next milestone, you'll use a group by transformation to aggregate your data.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Add field from calculation](/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/transform-data/#add-field-from-calculation)
- [Calculation types](/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/calculation-types/)
